config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh mudah dikorbankan atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menghapus martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan dasar tiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang menyeimbangkan kepentingan umum dengan hak individu, bukan menempatkan salah satunya sebagai korban tetap.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should not be subordinated to society as a default, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can become an excuse for abuse. Public safety and shared interests matter, but they have to be balanced against constitutional rights, due process, and personal liberty, not placed above them. I do not think a society is healthy if it treats individual rights as disposable whenever the majority wants something. The better principle is that society can limit rights only in narrow, clearly justified cases, and only with strong safeguards.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat ditempatkan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip utama. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal justru hak individu diperlukan agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, terutama untuk hal seperti kesehatan publik atau ketertiban, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, proporsional, dan tidak boleh menghapus martabat serta perlindungan dasar warga. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi keduanya secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, I am wary of any “common good” argument because history shows how quickly it can justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when the majority feels strongly. I do think there are narrow cases like public health or immediate safety where limited restrictions are justified, but those limits have to be temporary, proportionate, and tightly checked. A healthy society protects people by defending rights first, not by treating them as optional whenever collective pressure rises.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal justru hak individu dibutuhkan agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, terutama untuk kesehatan publik atau ketertiban, tetapi harus dibatasi secara ketat, proporsional, dan tidak boleh menghapus martabat serta perlindungan dasar warga. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi keduanya secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From a United States perspective, I am especially suspicious of that claim because “for the greater good” has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. Public health and safety can justify limited restrictions, but only when they are narrow, temporary, and heavily constrained by due process and constitutional protections. A healthy society protects the common good by respecting individual rights first, not by treating them as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
